# Savont Preliminary Results

[Jump to bottom](#wiki-pages-box)

Jim Shaw edited this page Jan 28, 2026
·
[12 revisions](/bluenote-1577/savont/wiki/Savont-Preliminary-Results/_history)

## Benchmark 1: ASV generation for Zymo Microbial Community Standard (D6300) ONT R10.4

We compared savont against unoise3 for ASV generation on a deeply sequenced ONT R10.4 dataset with sup basecalling.

* This dataset is the [Zymo D6300](https://zymoresearch.eu/collections/zymobiomics-microbial-community-standards/products/zymobiomics-microbial-community-standard) dataset, sequenced to ~1.5 million reads and ~69,000x depth per ASV.
* Reference ASVs were generated by UMI-based truths.
* We ran savont vs unoise. Unoise uses 8 minimum reads by default. If using lower threshold, you can essentially extrapolate the needed depth linearly (minimum 3 reads --> 3/8 times more sensitive).
* We downsampled the dataset to various fractions and reran each method.

### Precision vs sensitivity: how many false positive ASVs does savont produce?

![precision_sensitivity_nm_0](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/12787948/534322346-f5953f84-1d91-4f73-8fcb-b82003c1cf67.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..02su3PJTOG33HNh7EUMnbbdFH1MUInUahRwV9LnxB8E)

Tip

**Savont is far more sensitive than unoise at low coverage.** Unoise is very reliable at high coverage, both in terms of sensitivity and precision.

### Perfect ASV (0 mismatch) detection probability as a function of sequencing depth

![detection_probability_vs_depth_nm_0](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/12787948/534319641-19dbacad-2856-4888-a75e-0f1406f73265.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..ARUG8i-JtZYdAqJiqxIKhKjP4P1azK215AsDh8ux9Mw)

Tip

**Traditional ASV methods like unoise require > 1000x depth for reliable ASV generation**. Savont can generate ASVs with even > 30x depth reliably. Note: DADA2 did not finish running on our dataset.

### Species detection (i.e., detecting *at least one* ASV for a species) as a function of sequencing depth

![species_detection_probability_vs_depth](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/12787948/534320525-da891716-604b-44e3-839c-9ac307337dcb.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..iQ6xgRCWMLMBHkisYMf28kO0Gv6pMO_X-iSJocadmUw)

Tip

The interpretation of these two figures is that even if savont misses a few ASVs, it always detects *at least one ASV* per species.

In terms of species detection, savont is 100-1000x more sensitive than traditional ASV methods.

## Benchmark 2: Comparing savont to Emu for taxonomic profiling on Zymo Gut Microbiome Standard (D6331)

We took [Zymo D6331 dataset](https://zymoresearch.eu/products/zymobiomics-gut-microbiome-standard?srsltid=AfmBOorj5_1B0DevJoNn3DgTq1ToCDNliMKvzkE0emmxPH5APO0-UEiK) that was recently sequenced by Oxford Nanopore Technologies [using their new primers](https://epi2me.nanoporetech.com/zymo_16s_2025.09/).

We then compared savont against Emu, a read-based taxonomic profiler. ASV calls from savont were mapped to Emu's database.

### Savont and Emu have comparable abundances, but Emu has potential false-positive species calls

![emu_vs_savont_zymo_species_abundance](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/12787948/534323732-b099281d-c7e5-420a-965d-7d02345659aa.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..NLsyBlVJBhwx_SKJozDFjwn6nzn4pPNoAgVoz8MCAi4)

Tip

The red labels indicate species that should not be present in the dataset. They are either present at low abundance due to contamination or erroneously called by Emu. These red species (e.g. Shigella) have similar 16S sequences to the actual present species, hinting at misclassification instead of contamination.

Note

V. rogosae is in the community rather than V. parvula. However, their 16s sequences are similar, and the ASV is best classified as V. parvula according to Emu's database. BLASTN indeed gives the ASV as V. parvula.

### Savont is relatively fast and efficient (40 threads)

![emu_savont_performance_comparison](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/12787948/534324053-a54a2f26-0286-421b-9cae-42b71e5ba4f4.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..JH_KarzvX9LzSBnKC6cd4W9sdQoEEmh2Ii6bbEbWiEk)

## Benchmark 3 - How concordant are PacBio HiFi + unoise3 ASVs versus ONT + savont on real data?

We compared unoise + PacBio HiFi 16S full-length amplicons to ONT + savont on three samples. These samples had uniform DNA extraction and library prep for both technologies.

On these datasets, ONT + savont gave 1.5x to over 10x the number of ASVs compared to HiFi + unoise. We aligned HiFi + unoise ASVs to ONT + savont and plotted the number of mismatches.

![zotu_asv_mismatch_distribution_barplot](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/12787948/534399940-e3775f43-de08-4047-b70b-8164a0aba5c2.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.
